title: Contributor-onboarding docs for an under-documented tool
context: Many open-source cancer research tools lack a clear CONTRIBUTING.md or contributor-onboarding guide, which creates a high barrier for new contributors and drives maintainer burnout from answering repeated 'how do I contribute?' questions. Contributor-onboarding docs directly help the maintainer community grow and reduce the maintainer support burden — a direct benefit to the ecosystem. riskTier is low: onboarding docs are developer-facing, contain no biological claims, and require no cancer data.
objective: Author and contribute merged contributor-onboarding documentation for a gate-passed under-documented cancer research tool, covering the development setup, contribution workflow, DCO/CLA requirements, test-run instructions, and code-of-conduct reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Target tool has a PASS gate artifact confirming OSI-permissive or copyleft license and acceptable contribution policy.
Issue-first alignment confirmed with the maintainer; the onboarding docs gap is confirmed as wanted.
Contributor-onboarding docs cover: development environment setup, contribution workflow (fork/branch/PR), DCO or CLA requirement, how to run tests, and code-of-conduct reference.
No biological/clinical claims; no patient data; developer-facing only.
License + Data-provenance reviewer sign-off and Technical reviewer sign-off obtained.
Contribution merged upstream; outcome artifact committed at outcomes/onco-research-software-docs-contribguide-025.json with merge-commit URL.
resources: Gate artifacts from triage-009 or a new gate assessment for the target tool
output: Contributor-onboarding documentation merged upstream for a gate-passed under-documented cancer research tool, with outcome artifact recorded — helping the maintainer community grow and reducing maintainer support burden.
